   896→// handleLogsOnlyCheckout restores logs and checks out the commit (detached HEAD).
   897→func handleLogsOnlyCheckout(start strategy.Strategy, point strategy.RewindPoint, shortID string) error {
   898→	// Resolve agent once for use throughout
   899→	agent, err := getAgent(point.Agent)
   900→	if err != nil {
   901→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   902→	}
   903→
   904→	// Initialize logging context with agent from checkpoint
   905→	ctx := logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "rewind")
   906→	ctx = logging.WithAgent(ctx, agent.Name())
   907→
   908→	logging.Debug(ctx, "logs-only checkout started",
   909→		slog.String("checkpoint_id", point.ID),
   910→		slog.String("session_id", point.SessionID),
   911→	)
   912→
   913→	// First, restore the logs
   914→	restorer, ok := start.(strategy.LogsOnlyRestorer)
   915→	if !ok {
   916→		return errors.New("strategy does not support logs-only restoration")
   917→	}
   918→
   919→	sessions, err := restorer.RestoreLogsOnly(point, true) // force=true for explicit rewind
   920→	if err != nil {
   921→		logging.Error(ctx, "logs-only checkout failed during log restoration",
   922→			slog.String("checkpoint_id", point.ID),
   923→			slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   924→		)
   925→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to restore logs: %w", err)
   926→	}
   927→
   928→	// Show warning about detached HEAD
   929→	var confirm bool
   930→	confirmForm := NewAccessibleForm(
   931→		huh.NewGroup(
   932→			huh.NewConfirm().
   933→				Title("Create detached HEAD?").
   934→				Description("This will checkout the commit directly. You'll be in 'detached HEAD' state.\nAny uncommitted changes will be lost!").
   935→				Value(&confirm),
   936→		),
   937→	)
   938→
   939→	if err := confirmForm.Run(); err != nil {
   940→		return fmt.Errorf("confirmation cancelled: %w", err)
   941→	}
   942→
   943→	if !confirm {
   944→		fmt.Println("Checkout cancelled. Session logs were still restored.")
   945→		printMultiSessionResumeCommands(sessions)
   946→		return nil
   947→	}
   948→
   949→	// Perform git checkout
   950→	if err := CheckoutBranch(point.ID); err != nil {
   951→		logging.Error(ctx, "logs-only checkout failed during git checkout",
   952→			slog.String("checkpoint_id", point.ID),
   953→			slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   954→		)
   955→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to checkout commit: %w", err)
   956→	}
   957→
   958→	logging.Debug(ctx, "logs-only checkout completed",
   959→		slog.String("checkpoint_id", point.ID),
   960→	)
   961→
   962→	fmt.Printf("Checked out %s (detached HEAD).\n", shortID)
   963→	printMultiSessionResumeCommands(sessions)
   964→	return nil
   965→}

   967→// handleLogsOnlyReset restores logs and resets the branch to the commit (destructive).
   968→func handleLogsOnlyReset(start strategy.Strategy, point strategy.RewindPoint, shortID string) error {
   969→	// Resolve agent once for use throughout
   970→	agent, agentErr := getAgent(point.Agent)
   971→	if agentErr != nil {
   972→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", agentErr)
   973→	}
   974→
   975→	// Initialize logging context with agent from checkpoint
   976→	ctx := logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "rewind")
   977→	ctx = logging.WithAgent(ctx, agent.Name())
   978→
   979→	logging.Debug(ctx, "logs-only reset (interactive) started",
   980→		slog.String("checkpoint_id", point.ID),
   981→		slog.String("session_id", point.SessionID),
   982→	)
   983→
   984→	// First, restore the logs
   985→	restorer, ok := start.(strategy.LogsOnlyRestorer)
   986→	if !ok {
   987→		return errors.New("strategy does not support logs-only restoration")
   988→	}
   989→
   990→	sessions, restoreErr := restorer.RestoreLogsOnly(point, true) // force=true for explicit rewind
   991→	if restoreErr != nil {
   992→		logging.Error(ctx, "logs-only reset failed during log restoration",
   993→			slog.String("checkpoint_id", point.ID),
   994→			slog.String("error", restoreErr.Error()),
   995→		)
   996→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to restore logs: %w", restoreErr)
   997→	}
   998→
   999→	// Get current HEAD before reset (for recovery message)
  1000→	currentHead, err := getCurrentHeadHash()
  1001→	if err != nil {
  1002→		// Non-fatal - just won't show recovery message
  1003→		currentHead = ""
  1004→	}
  1005→
  1006→	// Get detailed uncommitted changes warning from strategy
  1007→	var uncommittedWarning string
  1008→	if _, warn, err := start.CanRewind(); err == nil {
  1009→		uncommittedWarning = warn
  1010→	}
  1011→
  1012→	// Check for safety issues
  1013→	warnings, err := checkResetSafety(point.ID, uncommittedWarning)
  1014→	if err != nil {
  1015→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to check reset safety: %w", err)
  1016→	}
  1017→
  1018→	// Build confirmation message based on warnings
  1019→	var confirmTitle, confirmDesc string
  1020→	if len(warnings) > 0 {
  1021→		confirmTitle = "⚠️  Reset branch with warnings?"
  1022→		confirmDesc = "WARNING - the following issues were detected:\n" +
  1023→			strings.Join(warnings, "\n") +
  1024→			"\n\nThis will move your branch to " + shortID + " and DISCARD commits after it!"
  1025→	} else {
  1026→		confirmTitle = "Reset branch to " + shortID + "?"
  1027→		confirmDesc = "This will move your branch pointer to this commit.\nCommits after this point will be orphaned (but recoverable via reflog)."
  1028→	}
  1029→
  1030→	var confirm bool
  1031→	confirmForm := NewAccessibleForm(
  1032→		huh.NewGroup(
  1033→			huh.NewConfirm().
  1034→				Title(confirmTitle).
  1035→				Description(confirmDesc).
  1036→				Value(&confirm),
  1037→		),
  1038→	)
  1039→
  1040→	if err := confirmForm.Run(); err != nil {
  1041→		return fmt.Errorf("confirmation cancelled: %w", err)
  1042→	}
  1043→
  1044→	if !confirm {
  1045→		fmt.Println("Reset cancelled. Session logs were still restored.")
  1046→		printMultiSessionResumeCommands(sessions)
  1047→		return nil
  1048→	}
  1049→
  1050→	// Perform git reset --hard
  1051→	if err := performGitResetHard(point.ID); err != nil {
  1052→		logging.Error(ctx, "logs-only reset failed during git reset",
  1053→			slog.String("checkpoint_id", point.ID),
  1054→			slog.String("error", err.Error()),
  1055→		)
  1056→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to reset branch: %w", err)
  1057→	}
  1058→
  1059→	logging.Debug(ctx, "logs-only reset (interactive) completed",
  1060→		slog.String("checkpoint_id", point.ID),
  1061→	)
  1062→
  1063→	fmt.Printf("Reset branch to %s.\n", shortID)
  1064→	printMultiSessionResumeCommands(sessions)
  1065→
  1066→	// Show recovery instructions
  1067→	if currentHead != "" && currentHead != point.ID {
  1068→		currentShort := currentHead
  1069→		if len(currentShort) > 7 {
  1070→			currentShort = currentShort[:7]
  1071→		}
  1072→		fmt.Printf("\nTo undo this reset: git reset --hard %s\n", currentShort)
  1073→	}
  1074→
  1075→	return nil
  1076→}
  1077→
  1078→// getCurrentHeadHash returns the current HEAD commit hash.
  1079→func getCurrentHeadHash() (string, error) {
  1080→	repo, err := openRepository()
  1081→	if err != nil {
  1082→		return "", err
  1083→	}
  1084→
  1085→	head, err := repo.Head()
  1086→	if err != nil {
  1087→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get HEAD: %w", err)
  1088→	}
  1089→
  1090→	return head.Hash().String(), nil
  1091→}
  1092→
  1093→// checkResetSafety checks for potential issues before a git reset --hard.
  1094→// Returns a list of warning messages (empty if safe to proceed without warnings).
  1095→// If uncommittedChangesWarning is provided, it will be used instead of a generic warning.
  1096→func checkResetSafety(targetCommitHash string, uncommittedChangesWarning string) ([]string, error) {
  1097→	var warnings []string
  1098→
  1099→	repo, err := openRepository()
  1100→	if err != nil {
  1101→		return nil, err
  1102→	}
  1103→
  1104→	// Check for uncommitted changes
  1105→	if uncommittedChangesWarning != "" {
  1106→		// Use the detailed warning from strategy's CanRewind()
  1107→		warnings = append(warnings, uncommittedChangesWarning)
  1108→	} else {
  1109→		// Fall back to generic check
  1110→		worktree, err := repo.Worktree()
  1111→		if err != nil {
  1112→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get worktree: %w", err)
  1113→		}
  1114→
  1115→		status, err := worktree.Status()
  1116→		if err != nil {
  1117→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get status: %w", err)
  1118→		}
  1119→
  1120→		if !status.IsClean() {
  1121→			warnings = append(warnings, "• You have uncommitted changes that will be LOST")
  1122→		}
  1123→	}
  1124→
  1125→	// Check if current HEAD is ahead of target (we'd be discarding commits)
  1126→	head, err := repo.Head()
  1127→	if err != nil {
  1128→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get HEAD: %w", err)
  1129→	}
  1130→
  1131→	targetHash := plumbing.NewHash(targetCommitHash)
  1132→
  1133→	// Count commits between target and HEAD
  1134→	commitsAhead, err := countCommitsBetween(repo, targetHash, head.Hash())
  1135→	if err != nil {
  1136→		// Non-fatal - just can't show commit count
  1137→		commitsAhead = -1
  1138→	}
  1139→
  1140→	if commitsAhead > 0 {
  1141→		warnings = append(warnings, fmt.Sprintf("• %d commit(s) after this point will be orphaned", commitsAhead))
  1142→	}
  1143→
  1144→	return warnings, nil
  1145→}
  1146→
  1147→// countCommitsBetween counts commits between ancestor and descendant.
  1148→// Returns 0 if ancestor == descendant, -1 on error.
  1149→func countCommitsBetween(repo *git.Repository, ancestor, descendant plumbing.Hash) (int, error) {
  1150→	if ancestor == descendant {
  1151→		return 0, nil
  1152→	}
  1153→
  1154→	// Walk from descendant back to ancestor
  1155→	count := 0
  1156→	current := descendant
  1157→
  1158→	for count < 1000 { // Safety limit
  1159→		if current == ancestor {
  1160→			return count, nil
  1161→		}
  1162→
  1163→		commit, err := repo.CommitObject(current)
  1164→		if err != nil {
  1165→			return -1, fmt.Errorf("failed to get commit: %w", err)
  1166→		}
  1167→
  1168→		if commit.NumParents() == 0 {
  1169→			// Reached root without finding ancestor - ancestor not in history
  1170→			return -1, nil
  1171→		}
  1172→
  1173→		count++
  1174→		current = commit.ParentHashes[0] // Follow first parent
  1175→	}
  1176→
  1177→	return -1, nil
  1178→}
  1179→
  1180→// performGitResetHard performs a git reset --hard to the specified commit.
  1181→// Uses the git CLI instead of go-git because go-git's HardReset incorrectly
  1182→// deletes untracked directories (like .entire/) even when they're in .gitignore.
  1183→func performGitResetHard(commitHash string) error {
  1184→	ctx := context.Background()
  1185→	cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", "reset", "--hard", commitHash)
  1186→	if output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
  1187→		return fmt.Errorf("reset failed: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(output)), err)
  1188→	}
  1189→	return nil
  1190→}
  1191→
  1192→// sanitizeForTerminal removes or replaces characters that cause rendering issues
  1193→// in terminal UI components. This includes emojis with skin-tone modifiers and
  1194→// other multi-codepoint characters that confuse width calculations.
  1195→func sanitizeForTerminal(s string) string {
  1196→	var result strings.Builder
  1197→	result.Grow(len(s))
  1198→
  1199→	for _, r := range s {
  1200→		// Skip emoji skin tone modifiers (U+1F3FB to U+1F3FF)
  1201→		if r >= 0x1F3FB && r <= 0x1F3FF {
  1202→			continue
  1203→		}
  1204→		// Skip zero-width joiners used in emoji sequences
  1205→		if r == 0x200D {
  1206→			continue
  1207→		}
  1208→		// Skip variation selectors (U+FE00 to U+FE0F)
  1209→		if r >= 0xFE00 && r <= 0xFE0F {
  1210→			continue
  1211→		}
  1212→		// Keep printable characters and common whitespace
  1213→		if unicode.IsPrint(r) || r == '\t' || r == '\n' {
  1214→			result.WriteRune(r)
  1215→		}
  1216→	}
  1217→
  1218→	return result.String()
  1219→}
  1220→
  1221→// printMultiSessionResumeCommands prints resume commands for restored sessions.
  1222→// Each session may have a different agent, so per-session agent resolution is used.
  1223→func printMultiSessionResumeCommands(sessions []strategy.RestoredSession) {
  1224→	if len(sessions) == 0 {
  1225→		return
  1226→	}
  1227→
  1228→	if len(sessions) > 1 {
  1229→		fmt.Printf("\nRestored %d sessions. Resume with:\n", len(sessions))
  1230→	}
  1231→
  1232→	for i, sess := range sessions {
  1233→		ag, err := strategy.ResolveAgentForRewind(sess.Agent)
  1234→		if err != nil {
  1235→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  Warning: could not resolve agent %q for session %s, skipping\n", sess.Agent, sess.SessionID)
  1236→			continue
  1237→		}
  1238→
  1239→		cmd := ag.FormatResumeCommand(sess.SessionID)
  1240→
  1241→		if len(sessions) > 1 {
  1242→			// Add "(most recent)" label to the last session
  1243→			if i == len(sessions)-1 {
  1244→				if sess.Prompt != "" {
  1245→					fmt.Printf("  %s  # %s (most recent)\n", cmd, sess.Prompt)
  1246→				} else {
  1247→					fmt.Printf("  %s  # (most recent)\n", cmd)
  1248→				}
  1249→			} else {
  1250→				if sess.Prompt != "" {
  1251→					fmt.Printf("  %s  # %s\n", cmd, sess.Prompt)
  1252→				} else {
  1253→					fmt.Printf("  %s\n", cmd)
  1254→				}
  1255→			}
  1256→		} else {
  1257→			if sess.Prompt != "" {
  1258→				fmt.Printf("%s  # %s\n", cmd, sess.Prompt)
  1259→			} else {
  1260→				fmt.Printf("%s\n", cmd)
  1261→			}
  1262→		}
  1263→	}
  1264→}

    30→// listCheckpoints returns all checkpoints from the metadata branch.
    31→// Uses checkpoint.GitStore.ListCommitted() for reading from entire/checkpoints/v1.
    32→func (s *ManualCommitStrategy) listCheckpoints() ([]CheckpointInfo, error) {
    33→	store, err := s.getCheckpointStore()
    34→	if err != nil {
    35→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get checkpoint store: %w", err)
    36→	}
    37→
    38→	committed, err := store.ListCommitted(context.Background())
    39→	if err != nil {
    40→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to list committed checkpoints: %w", err)
    41→	}
    42→
    43→	// Convert from checkpoint.CommittedInfo to strategy.CheckpointInfo
    44→	result := make([]CheckpointInfo, 0, len(committed))
    45→	for _, c := range committed {
    46→		result = append(result, CheckpointInfo{
    47→			CheckpointID:     c.CheckpointID,
    48→			SessionID:        c.SessionID,
    49→			CreatedAt:        c.CreatedAt,
    50→			CheckpointsCount: c.CheckpointsCount,
    51→			FilesTouched:     c.FilesTouched,
    52→			Agent:            c.Agent,
    53→			IsTask:           c.IsTask,
    54→			ToolUseID:        c.ToolUseID,
    55→			SessionCount:     c.SessionCount,
    56→			SessionIDs:       c.SessionIDs,
    57→		})
    58→	}
    59→
    60→	return result, nil
    61→}
    62→
    63→// getCheckpointsForSession returns all checkpoints for a session ID.
    64→func (s *ManualCommitStrategy) getCheckpointsForSession(sessionID string) ([]CheckpointInfo, error) {
    65→	all, err := s.listCheckpoints()
    66→	if err != nil {
    67→		return nil, err
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	var result []CheckpointInfo
    71→	for _, cp := range all {
    72→		if cp.SessionID == sessionID || strings.HasPrefix(cp.SessionID, sessionID) {
    73→			result = append(result, cp)
    74→		}
    75→	}
    76→
    77→	if len(result) == 0 {
    78→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no checkpoints for session: %s", sessionID)
    79→	}
    80→
    81→	return result, nil
    82→}
    83→
    84→// getCheckpointLog returns the transcript for a specific checkpoint ID.
    85→// Uses checkpoint.GitStore.ReadCommitted() for reading from entire/checkpoints/v1.
    86→func (s *ManualCommitStrategy) getCheckpointLog(checkpointID id.CheckpointID) ([]byte, error) {
    87→	store, err := s.getCheckpointStore()
    88→	if err != nil {
    89→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get checkpoint store: %w", err)
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	content, err := store.ReadLatestSessionContent(context.Background(), checkpointID)
    93→	if err != nil {
    94→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read checkpoint: %w", err)
    95→	}
    96→	if content == nil {
    97→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("checkpoint not found: %s", checkpointID)
    98→	}
    99→	if len(content.Transcript) == 0 {
   100→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no transcript found for checkpoint: %s", checkpointID)
   101→	}
   102→
   103→	return content.Transcript, nil
   104→}
   105→
   106→// CondenseSession condenses a session's shadow branch to permanent storage.
   107→// checkpointID is the 12-hex-char value from the Entire-Checkpoint trailer.
   108→// Metadata is stored at sharded path: <checkpoint_id[:2]>/<checkpoint_id[2:]>/
   109→// Uses checkpoint.GitStore.WriteCommitted for the git operations.
   110→//
   111→// For mid-session commits (no Stop/SaveChanges called yet), the shadow branch may not exist.
   112→// In this case, data is extracted from the live transcript instead.
   113→func (s *ManualCommitStrategy) CondenseSession(repo *git.Repository, checkpointID id.CheckpointID, state *SessionState, committedFiles map[string]struct{}) (*CondenseResult, error) {
   114→	// Get shadow branch (may not exist for mid-session commits)
   115→	shadowBranchName := getShadowBranchNameForCommit(state.BaseCommit, state.WorktreeID)
   116→	refName := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(shadowBranchName)
   117→	ref, err := repo.Reference(refName, true)
   118→	hasShadowBranch := err == nil
   119→
   120→	var sessionData *ExtractedSessionData
   121→	if hasShadowBranch {
   122→		// Extract session data from the shadow branch (with live transcript fallback).
   123→		// Use tracked files from session state instead of collecting all files from tree.
   124→		// Pass agent type to handle different transcript formats (JSONL for Claude, JSON for Gemini).
   125→		// Pass live transcript path so condensation reads the current file rather than a
   126→		// potentially stale shadow branch copy (SaveChanges may have been skipped if the
   127→		// last turn had no code changes).
   128→		// Pass CheckpointTranscriptStart for accurate token calculation (line offset for Claude, message index for Gemini).
   129→		sessionData, err = s.extractSessionData(repo, ref.Hash(), state.SessionID, state.FilesTouched, state.AgentType, state.TranscriptPath, state.CheckpointTranscriptStart)
   130→		if err != nil {
   131→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to extract session data: %w", err)
   132→		}
   133→	} else {
   134→		// No shadow branch: mid-session commit before Stop/SaveChanges.
   135→		// Extract data directly from live transcript.
   136→		if state.TranscriptPath == "" {
   137→			return nil, errors.New("shadow branch not found and no live transcript available")
   138→		}
   139→		sessionData, err = s.extractSessionDataFromLiveTranscript(state)
   140→		if err != nil {
   141→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to extract session data from live transcript: %w", err)
   142→		}
   143→	}
   144→
   145→	// For 1:1 checkpoint model: filter files_touched to only include files actually
   146→	// committed in this specific commit. This ensures each checkpoint represents
   147→	// exactly the files in that commit, not all files mentioned in the transcript.
   148→	if len(committedFiles) > 0 {
   149→		if len(sessionData.FilesTouched) > 0 {
   150→			// Filter to intersection of transcript-extracted files and committed files
   151→			filtered := make([]string, 0, len(sessionData.FilesTouched))
   152→			for _, f := range sessionData.FilesTouched {
   153→				if _, ok := committedFiles[f]; ok {
   154→					filtered = append(filtered, f)
   155→				}
   156→			}
   157→			sessionData.FilesTouched = filtered
   158→		}
   159→
   160→		// If extraction failed or returned empty, use committedFiles as fallback.
   161→		// This handles mid-session commits where transcript parsing may not find files
   162→		// but we know what was committed.
   163→		if len(sessionData.FilesTouched) == 0 {
   164→			sessionData.FilesTouched = make([]string, 0, len(committedFiles))
   165→			for f := range committedFiles {
   166→				sessionData.FilesTouched = append(sessionData.FilesTouched, f)
   167→			}
   168→		}
   169→	}
   170→
   171→	// Get checkpoint store
   172→	store, err := s.getCheckpointStore()
   173→	if err != nil {
   174→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get checkpoint store: %w", err)
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	// Get author info
   178→	authorName, authorEmail := GetGitAuthorFromRepo(repo)
   179→	// Attribution calculation requires shadow branch reference; skip if mid-session commit
   180→	var attribution *cpkg.InitialAttribution
   181→	if hasShadowBranch {
   182→		attribution = calculateSessionAttributions(repo, ref, sessionData, state)
   183→	}
   184→	// Get current branch name
   185→	branchName := GetCurrentBranchName(repo)
   186→
   187→	// Generate summary if enabled
   188→	var summary *cpkg.Summary
   189→	if settings.IsSummarizeEnabled() && len(sessionData.Transcript) > 0 {
   190→		logCtx := logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "attribution")
   191→		summarizeCtx := logging.WithComponent(logCtx, "summarize")
   192→
   193→		// Scope transcript to this checkpoint's portion.
   194→		// For Claude Code (JSONL), CheckpointTranscriptStart is a line offset.
   195→		// For Gemini (JSON), CheckpointTranscriptStart is a message index.
   196→		var scopedTranscript []byte
   197→		if state.AgentType == agent.AgentTypeGemini {
   198→			scopedTranscript = geminicli.SliceFromMessage(sessionData.Transcript, state.CheckpointTranscriptStart)
   199→		} else {
   200→			scopedTranscript = transcript.SliceFromLine(sessionData.Transcript, state.CheckpointTranscriptStart)
   201→		}
   202→		if len(scopedTranscript) > 0 {
   203→			var err error
   204→			summary, err = summarize.GenerateFromTranscript(summarizeCtx, scopedTranscript, sessionData.FilesTouched, state.AgentType, nil)
   205→			if err != nil {
   206→				logging.Warn(summarizeCtx, "summary generation failed",
   207→					slog.String("session_id", state.SessionID),
   208→					slog.String("error", err.Error()))
   209→				// Continue without summary - non-blocking
   210→			} else {
   211→				logging.Info(summarizeCtx, "summary generated",
   212→					slog.String("session_id", state.SessionID))
   213→			}
   214→		}
   215→	}
   216→
   217→	// Write checkpoint metadata using the checkpoint store
   218→	if err := store.WriteCommitted(context.Background(), cpkg.WriteCommittedOptions{
   219→		CheckpointID:                checkpointID,
   220→		SessionID:                   state.SessionID,
   221→		Strategy:                    StrategyNameManualCommit,
   222→		Branch:                      branchName,
   223→		Transcript:                  sessionData.Transcript,
   224→		Prompts:                     sessionData.Prompts,
   225→		Context:                     sessionData.Context,
   226→		FilesTouched:                sessionData.FilesTouched,
   227→		CheckpointsCount:            state.StepCount,
   228→		EphemeralBranch:             shadowBranchName,
   229→		AuthorName:                  authorName,
   230→		AuthorEmail:                 authorEmail,
   231→		Agent:                       state.AgentType,
   232→		TurnID:                      state.TurnID,
   233→		TranscriptIdentifierAtStart: state.TranscriptIdentifierAtStart,
   234→		CheckpointTranscriptStart:   state.CheckpointTranscriptStart,
   235→		TokenUsage:                  sessionData.TokenUsage,
   236→		InitialAttribution:          attribution,
   237→		Summary:                     summary,
   238→		SessionTranscriptPath:       homeRelativePath(state.TranscriptPath),
   239→	}); err != nil {
   240→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to write checkpoint metadata: %w", err)
   241→	}
   242→
   243→	return &CondenseResult{
   244→		CheckpointID:         checkpointID,
   245→		SessionID:            state.SessionID,
   246→		CheckpointsCount:     state.StepCount,
   247→		FilesTouched:         sessionData.FilesTouched,
   248→		TotalTranscriptLines: sessionData.FullTranscriptLines,
   249→	}, nil
   250→}
   251→
   252→func calculateSessionAttributions(repo *git.Repository, shadowRef *plumbing.Reference, sessionData *ExtractedSessionData, state *SessionState) *cpkg.InitialAttribution {
   253→	// Calculate initial attribution using accumulated prompt attribution data.
   254→	// This uses user edits captured at each prompt start (before agent works),
   255→	// plus any user edits after the final checkpoint (shadow → head).
   256→	logCtx := logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "attribution")
   257→	var attribution *cpkg.InitialAttribution
   258→	headRef, headErr := repo.Head()
   259→	if headErr != nil {
   260→		logging.Debug(logCtx, "attribution skipped: failed to get HEAD",
   261→			slog.String("error", headErr.Error()))
   262→	} else {
   263→		headCommit, commitErr := repo.CommitObject(headRef.Hash())
   264→		if commitErr != nil {
   265→			logging.Debug(logCtx, "attribution skipped: failed to get HEAD commit",
   266→				slog.String("error", commitErr.Error()))
   267→		} else {
   268→			headTree, treeErr := headCommit.Tree()
   269→			if treeErr != nil {
   270→				logging.Debug(logCtx, "attribution skipped: failed to get HEAD tree",
   271→					slog.String("error", treeErr.Error()))
   272→			} else {
   273→				// Get shadow branch tree (checkpoint tree - what the agent wrote)
   274→				shadowCommit, shadowErr := repo.CommitObject(shadowRef.Hash())
   275→				if shadowErr != nil {
   276→					logging.Debug(logCtx, "attribution skipped: failed to get shadow commit",
   277→						slog.String("error", shadowErr.Error()),
   278→						slog.String("shadow_ref", shadowRef.Hash().String()))
   279→				} else {
   280→					shadowTree, shadowTreeErr := shadowCommit.Tree()
   281→					if shadowTreeErr != nil {
   282→						logging.Debug(logCtx, "attribution skipped: failed to get shadow tree",
   283→							slog.String("error", shadowTreeErr.Error()))
   284→					} else {
   285→						// Get base tree (state before session started)
   286→						var baseTree *object.Tree
   287→						attrBase := state.AttributionBaseCommit
   288→						if attrBase == "" {
   289→							attrBase = state.BaseCommit // backward compat
   290→						}
   291→						if baseCommit, baseErr := repo.CommitObject(plumbing.NewHash(attrBase)); baseErr == nil {
   292→							if tree, baseTErr := baseCommit.Tree(); baseTErr == nil {
   293→								baseTree = tree
   294→							} else {
   295→								logging.Debug(logCtx, "attribution: base tree unavailable",
   296→									slog.String("error", baseTErr.Error()))
   297→							}
   298→						} else {
   299→							logging.Debug(logCtx, "attribution: base commit unavailable",
   300→								slog.String("error", baseErr.Error()),
   301→								slog.String("attribution_base", attrBase))
   302→						}
   303→
   304→						// Log accumulated prompt attributions for debugging
   305→						var totalUserAdded, totalUserRemoved int
   306→						for i, pa := range state.PromptAttributions {
   307→							totalUserAdded += pa.UserLinesAdded
   308→							totalUserRemoved += pa.UserLinesRemoved
   309→							logging.Debug(logCtx, "prompt attribution data",
   310→								slog.Int("checkpoint", pa.CheckpointNumber),
   311→								slog.Int("user_added", pa.UserLinesAdded),
   312→								slog.Int("user_removed", pa.UserLinesRemoved),
   313→								slog.Int("agent_added", pa.AgentLinesAdded),
   314→								slog.Int("agent_removed", pa.AgentLinesRemoved),
   315→								slog.Int("index", i))
   316→						}
   317→
   318→						attribution = CalculateAttributionWithAccumulated(
   319→							baseTree,
   320→							shadowTree,
   321→							headTree,
   322→							sessionData.FilesTouched,
   323→							state.PromptAttributions,
   324→						)
   325→
   326→						if attribution != nil {
   327→							logging.Info(logCtx, "attribution calculated",
   328→								slog.Int("agent_lines", attribution.AgentLines),
   329→								slog.Int("human_added", attribution.HumanAdded),
   330→								slog.Int("human_modified", attribution.HumanModified),
   331→								slog.Int("human_removed", attribution.HumanRemoved),
   332→								slog.Int("total_committed", attribution.TotalCommitted),
   333→								slog.Float64("agent_percentage", attribution.AgentPercentage),
   334→								slog.Int("accumulated_user_added", totalUserAdded),
   335→								slog.Int("accumulated_user_removed", totalUserRemoved),
   336→								slog.Int("files_touched", len(sessionData.FilesTouched)))
   337→						}
   338→					}
   339→				}
   340→			}
   341→		}
   342→	}
   343→	return attribution
   344→}
   345→
   346→// extractSessionData extracts session data from the shadow branch.
   347→// filesTouched is the list of files tracked during the session (from SessionState.FilesTouched).
   348→// agentType identifies the agent (e.g., "Gemini CLI", "Claude Code") to determine transcript format.
   349→// liveTranscriptPath, when non-empty and readable, is preferred over the shadow branch copy.
   350→// This handles the case where SaveChanges was skipped (no code changes) but the transcript
   351→// continued growing — the shadow branch copy would be stale.
   352→// checkpointTranscriptStart is the line offset (Claude) or message index (Gemini) where the current checkpoint began.
   353→func (s *ManualCommitStrategy) extractSessionData(repo *git.Repository, shadowRef plumbing.Hash, sessionID string, filesTouched []string, agentType agent.AgentType, liveTranscriptPath string, checkpointTranscriptStart int) (*ExtractedSessionData, error) {
   354→	commit, err := repo.CommitObject(shadowRef)
   355→	if err != nil {
   356→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get commit object: %w", err)
   357→	}
   358→
   359→	tree, err := commit.Tree()
   360→	if err != nil {
   361→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get commit tree: %w", err)
   362→	}
   363→
   364→	data := &ExtractedSessionData{}
   365→	// sessionID is already an "entire session ID" (with date prefix)
   366→	metadataDir := paths.SessionMetadataDirFromSessionID(sessionID)
   367→
   368→	// Extract transcript — prefer the live file when available, fall back to shadow branch.
   369→	// The shadow branch copy may be stale if the last turn ended without code changes
   370→	// (SaveChanges is only called when there are file modifications).
   371→	var fullTranscript string
   372→	if liveTranscriptPath != "" {
   373→		if liveData, readErr := os.ReadFile(liveTranscriptPath); readErr == nil && len(liveData) > 0 { //nolint:gosec // path from session state
   374→			fullTranscript = string(liveData)
   375→		}
   376→	}
   377→	if fullTranscript == "" {
   378→		// Fall back to shadow branch copy
   379→		if file, fileErr := tree.File(metadataDir + "/" + paths.TranscriptFileName); fileErr == nil {
   380→			if content, contentErr := file.Contents(); contentErr == nil {
   381→				fullTranscript = content
   382→			}
   383→		} else if file, fileErr := tree.File(metadataDir + "/" + paths.TranscriptFileNameLegacy); fileErr == nil {
   384→			if content, contentErr := file.Contents(); contentErr == nil {
   385→				fullTranscript = content
   386→			}
   387→		}
   388→	}
   389→
   390→	// Process transcript based on agent type
   391→	if fullTranscript != "" {
   392→		data.Transcript = []byte(fullTranscript)
   393→		data.FullTranscriptLines = countTranscriptItems(agentType, fullTranscript)
   394→		data.Prompts = extractUserPrompts(agentType, fullTranscript)
   395→		data.Context = generateContextFromPrompts(data.Prompts)
   396→	}
   397→
   398→	// Use tracked files from session state (not all files in tree)
   399→	data.FilesTouched = filesTouched
   400→
   401→	// Calculate token usage from the extracted transcript portion
   402→	if len(data.Transcript) > 0 {
   403→		data.TokenUsage = calculateTokenUsage(agentType, data.Transcript, checkpointTranscriptStart)
   404→	}
   405→
   406→	return data, nil
   407→}
   408→
   409→// extractSessionDataFromLiveTranscript extracts session data directly from the live transcript file.
   410→// This is used for mid-session commits where no shadow branch exists yet.
   411→func (s *ManualCommitStrategy) extractSessionDataFromLiveTranscript(state *SessionState) (*ExtractedSessionData, error) {
   412→	data := &ExtractedSessionData{}
   413→
   414→	// Read the live transcript
   415→	if state.TranscriptPath == "" {
   416→		return nil, errors.New("no transcript path in session state")
   417→	}
   418→
   419→	liveData, err := os.ReadFile(state.TranscriptPath)
   420→	if err != nil {
   421→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read live transcript: %w", err)
   422→	}
   423→
   424→	if len(liveData) == 0 {
   425→		return nil, errors.New("live transcript is empty")
   426→	}
   427→
   428→	fullTranscript := string(liveData)
   429→	data.Transcript = liveData
   430→	data.FullTranscriptLines = countTranscriptItems(state.AgentType, fullTranscript)
   431→	data.Prompts = extractUserPrompts(state.AgentType, fullTranscript)
   432→	data.Context = generateContextFromPrompts(data.Prompts)
   433→
   434→	// Extract files from transcript since state.FilesTouched may be empty for mid-session commits
   435→	// (no SaveChanges/Stop has been called yet to populate it)
   436→	if len(state.FilesTouched) > 0 {
   437→		data.FilesTouched = state.FilesTouched
   438→	} else {
   439→		// Use the shared helper which includes subagent transcripts
   440→		data.FilesTouched = s.extractModifiedFilesFromLiveTranscript(state, state.CheckpointTranscriptStart)
   441→	}
   442→
   443→	// Calculate token usage from the extracted transcript portion
   444→	if len(data.Transcript) > 0 {
   445→		data.TokenUsage = calculateTokenUsage(state.AgentType, data.Transcript, state.CheckpointTranscriptStart)
   446→	}
   447→
   448→	return data, nil
   449→}
   450→
   451→// countTranscriptItems counts lines (JSONL) or messages (JSON) in a transcript.
   452→// For Claude Code and JSONL-based agents, this counts lines.
   453→// For Gemini CLI and JSON-based agents, this counts messages.
   454→// Returns 0 if the content is empty or malformed.
   455→func countTranscriptItems(agentType agent.AgentType, content string) int {
   456→	if content == "" {
   457→		return 0
   458→	}
   459→
   460→	// Try Gemini format first if agentType is Gemini, or as fallback if Unknown
   461→	if agentType == agent.AgentTypeGemini || agentType == agent.AgentTypeUnknown {
   462→		transcript, err := geminicli.ParseTranscript([]byte(content))
   463→		if err == nil && transcript != nil && len(transcript.Messages) > 0 {
   464→			return len(transcript.Messages)
   465→		}
   466→		// If agentType is explicitly Gemini but parsing failed, return 0
   467→		if agentType == agent.AgentTypeGemini {
   468→			return 0
   469→		}
   470→		// Otherwise fall through to JSONL parsing for Unknown type
   471→	}
   472→
   473→	// Claude Code and other JSONL-based agents
   474→	allLines := strings.Split(content, "\n")
   475→	// Trim trailing empty lines (from final \n in JSONL)
   476→	for len(allLines) > 0 && strings.TrimSpace(allLines[len(allLines)-1]) == "" {
   477→		allLines = allLines[:len(allLines)-1]
   478→	}
   479→	return len(allLines)
   480→}
   481→
   482→// extractUserPrompts extracts all user prompts from transcript content.
   483→// Returns prompts with IDE context tags stripped (e.g., <ide_opened_file>).
   484→func extractUserPrompts(agentType agent.AgentType, content string) []string {
   485→	if content == "" {
   486→		return nil
   487→	}
   488→
   489→	// Try Gemini format first if agentType is Gemini, or as fallback if Unknown
   490→	if agentType == agent.AgentTypeGemini || agentType == agent.AgentTypeUnknown {
   491→		prompts, err := geminicli.ExtractAllUserPrompts([]byte(content))
   492→		if err == nil && len(prompts) > 0 {
   493→			// Strip IDE context tags for consistency with Claude Code handling
   494→			cleaned := make([]string, 0, len(prompts))
   495→			for _, prompt := range prompts {
   496→				if stripped := textutil.StripIDEContextTags(prompt); stripped != "" {
   497→					cleaned = append(cleaned, stripped)
   498→				}
   499→			}
   500→			return cleaned
   501→		}
   502→		// If agentType is explicitly Gemini but parsing failed, return nil
   503→		if agentType == agent.AgentTypeGemini {
   504→			return nil
   505→		}
   506→		// Otherwise fall through to JSONL parsing for Unknown type
   507→	}
   508→
   509→	// Claude Code and other JSONL-based agents
   510→	return extractUserPromptsFromLines(strings.Split(content, "\n"))
   511→}
   512→
   513→// calculateTokenUsage calculates token usage from raw transcript data.
   514→// startOffset is the line number (Claude Code) or message index (Gemini CLI)
   515→// where the current checkpoint began, allowing calculation for only the portion
   516→// of the transcript since the last checkpoint.
   517→func calculateTokenUsage(agentType agent.AgentType, data []byte, startOffset int) *agent.TokenUsage {
   518→	if len(data) == 0 {
   519→		return &agent.TokenUsage{}
   520→	}
   521→
   522→	// Try Gemini format first if agentType is Gemini, or as fallback if Unknown
   523→	if agentType == agent.AgentTypeGemini || agentType == agent.AgentTypeUnknown {
   524→		// Attempt to parse as Gemini JSON
   525→		transcript, err := geminicli.ParseTranscript(data)
   526→		if err == nil && transcript != nil && len(transcript.Messages) > 0 {
   527→			return geminicli.CalculateTokenUsage(data, startOffset)
   528→		}
   529→		// If agentType is explicitly Gemini but parsing failed, return empty usage
   530→		if agentType == agent.AgentTypeGemini {
   531→			return &agent.TokenUsage{}
   532→		}
   533→		// Otherwise fall through to JSONL parsing for Unknown type
   534→	}
   535→
   536→	// Claude Code and other JSONL-based agents
   537→	lines, err := claudecode.ParseTranscript(data)
   538→	if err != nil || len(lines) == 0 {
   539→		return &agent.TokenUsage{}
   540→	}
   541→	// Slice transcript lines to only include checkpoint portion
   542→	if startOffset > 0 && startOffset < len(lines) {
   543→		lines = lines[startOffset:]
   544→	}
   545→	return claudecode.CalculateTokenUsage(lines)
   546→}
   547→
   548→// extractUserPromptsFromLines extracts user prompts from JSONL transcript lines.
   549→// IDE-injected context tags (like <ide_opened_file>) are stripped from the results.
   550→func extractUserPromptsFromLines(lines []string) []string {
   551→	var prompts []string
   552→	for _, line := range lines {
   553→		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
   554→		if line == "" {
   555→			continue
   556→		}
   557→
   558→		var entry map[string]interface{}
   559→		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry); err != nil {
   560→			continue
   561→		}
   562→
   563→		// Check for user message (supports both "human" and "user" types)
   564→		msgType, ok := entry["type"].(string)
   565→		if !ok || (msgType != "human" && msgType != "user") {
   566→			continue
   567→		}
   568→
   569→		// Extract message content
   570→		message, ok := entry["message"].(map[string]interface{})
   571→		if !ok {
   572→			continue
   573→		}
   574→
   575→		// Handle string content
   576→		if content, ok := message["content"].(string); ok && content != "" {
   577→			cleaned := textutil.StripIDEContextTags(content)
   578→			if cleaned != "" {
   579→				prompts = append(prompts, cleaned)
   580→			}
   581→			continue
   582→		}
   583→
   584→		// Handle array content (e.g., multiple text blocks from VSCode)
   585→		if arr, ok := message["content"].([]interface{}); ok {
   586→			var texts []string
   587→			for _, item := range arr {
   588→				if m, ok := item.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
   589→					if m["type"] == "text" {
   590→						if text, ok := m["text"].(string); ok {
   591→							texts = append(texts, text)
   592→						}
   593→					}
   594→				}
   595→			}
   596→			if len(texts) > 0 {
   597→				cleaned := textutil.StripIDEContextTags(strings.Join(texts, "\n\n"))
   598→				if cleaned != "" {
   599→					prompts = append(prompts, cleaned)
   600→				}
   601→			}
   602→		}
   603→	}
   604→	return prompts
   605→}
   606→
   607→// generateContextFromPrompts generates context.md content from a list of prompts.
   608→func generateContextFromPrompts(prompts []string) []byte {
   609→	if len(prompts) == 0 {
   610→		return nil
   611→	}
   612→
   613→	var buf strings.Builder
   614→	buf.WriteString("# Session Context\n\n")
   615→	buf.WriteString("## User Prompts\n\n")
   616→
   617→	for i, prompt := range prompts {
   618→		// Truncate very long prompts for readability
   619→		displayPrompt := prompt
   620→		if len(displayPrompt) > 500 {
   621→			displayPrompt = displayPrompt[:500] + "..."
   622→		}
   623→		buf.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("### Prompt %d\n\n", i+1))
   624→		buf.WriteString(displayPrompt)
   625→		buf.WriteString("\n\n")
   626→	}
   627→
   628→	return []byte(buf.String())
   629→}
   630→
   631→// CondenseSessionByID force-condenses a session by its ID and cleans up.
   632→// This is used by "entire doctor" to salvage stuck sessions.
   633→func (s *ManualCommitStrategy) CondenseSessionByID(sessionID string) error {
   634→	ctx := logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "condense-by-id")
   635→
   636→	// Load session state
   637→	state, err := s.loadSessionState(sessionID)
   638→	if err != nil {
   639→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load session state: %w", err)
   640→	}
   641→	if state == nil {
   642→		return fmt.Errorf("session not found: %s", sessionID)
   643→	}
   644→
   645→	// Open repository
   646→	repo, err := OpenRepository()
   647→	if err != nil {
   648→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to open repository: %w", err)
   649→	}
   650→
   651→	// Generate a checkpoint ID
   652→	checkpointID, err := id.Generate()
   653→	if err != nil {
   654→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to generate checkpoint ID: %w", err)
   655→	}
   656→
   657→	// Check if shadow branch exists (required for condensation)
   658→	shadowBranchName := getShadowBranchNameForCommit(state.BaseCommit, state.WorktreeID)
   659→	refName := plumbing.NewBranchReferenceName(shadowBranchName)
   660→	_, refErr := repo.Reference(refName, true)
   661→	hasShadowBranch := refErr == nil
   662→
   663→	if !hasShadowBranch {
   664→		// No shadow branch means no checkpoint data to condense.
   665→		// Just clean up the state file.
   666→		logging.Info(ctx, "no shadow branch for session, clearing state only",
   667→			slog.String("session_id", sessionID),
   668→			slog.String("shadow_branch", shadowBranchName),
   669→		)
   670→		if err := s.clearSessionState(sessionID); err != nil {
   671→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to clear session state: %w", err)
   672→		}
   673→		return nil
   674→	}
   675→
   676→	// Condense the session
   677→	result, err := s.CondenseSession(repo, checkpointID, state, nil)
   678→	if err != nil {
   679→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to condense session: %w", err)
   680→	}
   681→
   682→	logging.Info(ctx, "session condensed by ID",
   683→		slog.String("session_id", sessionID),
   684→		slog.String("checkpoint_id", result.CheckpointID.String()),
   685→		slog.Int("checkpoints_condensed", result.CheckpointsCount),
   686→	)
   687→
   688→	// Update session state: reset step count and transition to idle
   689→	state.StepCount = 0
   690→	state.CheckpointTranscriptStart = result.TotalTranscriptLines
   691→	state.Phase = session.PhaseIdle
   692→	state.LastCheckpointID = checkpointID
   693→	state.AttributionBaseCommit = state.BaseCommit
   694→	state.PromptAttributions = nil
   695→	state.PendingPromptAttribution = nil
   696→
   697→	if err := s.saveSessionState(state); err != nil {
   698→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save session state: %w", err)
   699→	}
   700→
   701→	// Clean up shadow branch if no other sessions need it
   702→	if err := s.cleanupShadowBranchIfUnused(repo, shadowBranchName, sessionID); err != nil {
   703→		logging.Warn(ctx, "failed to clean up shadow branch",
   704→			slog.String("shadow_branch", shadowBranchName),
   705→			slog.String("error", err.Error()),
   706→		)
   707→		// Non-fatal: condensation succeeded, shadow branch cleanup is best-effort
   708→	}
   709→
   710→	return nil
   711→}
   712→
   713→// cleanupShadowBranchIfUnused deletes a shadow branch if no other active sessions reference it.
   714→func (s *ManualCommitStrategy) cleanupShadowBranchIfUnused(_ *git.Repository, shadowBranchName, excludeSessionID string) error {
   715→	// List all session states to check if any other session uses this shadow branch
   716→	allStates, err := s.listAllSessionStates()
   717→	if err != nil {
   718→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to list session states: %w", err)
   719→	}
   720→
   721→	for _, state := range allStates {
   722→		if state.SessionID == excludeSessionID {
   723→			continue
   724→		}
   725→		otherShadow := getShadowBranchNameForCommit(state.BaseCommit, state.WorktreeID)
   726→		if otherShadow == shadowBranchName && state.StepCount > 0 {
   727→			// Another session still needs this shadow branch
   728→			return nil
   729→		}
   730→	}
   731→
   732→	// No other sessions need it, delete the shadow branch via CLI
   733→	// (go-git v5's RemoveReference doesn't persist with packed refs/worktrees)
   734→	if err := DeleteBranchCLI(shadowBranchName); err != nil {
   735→		// Branch already gone is not an error
   736→		if errors.Is(err, ErrBranchNotFound) {
   737→			return nil
   738→		}
   739→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to remove shadow branch: %w", err)
   740→	}
   741→	return nil
   742→}
   743→

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Go transcript JSONL parsing with `bufio.Reader` approach (no size limit) vs old `bufio.Scanner` with 10MB buffer
   - Strategy pattern for session management (manual-commit, auto-commit)
   - Package import shadowing (local variable `transcript` shadowing `transcript` package)
   - Type aliases (`transcriptLine = transcript.Line`, `TranscriptLine = transcript.Line`)

   - **`cmd/entire/cli/transcript/parse.go`** — The shared transcript package where the new function lives.
     - Added `"os"` import
     - Added `ParseFromFileAtLine(path string, startLine int) ([]Line, int, error)` — the new consolidated function
     ```go
     func ParseFromFileAtLine(path string, startLine int) ([]Line, int, error) {
         file, err := os.Open(path) //nolint:gosec // path is a controlled transcript file path
         if err != nil {
             return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to open transcript: %w", err)
         }
         defer func() { _ = file.Close() }()
         var lines []Line
         reader := bufio.NewReader(file)
         totalLines := 0
         for {
             lineBytes, err := reader.ReadBytes('\n')
             if err != nil && err != io.EOF {
                 return nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to read transcript: %w", err)
             }
             if len(lineBytes) == 0 {
                 if err == io.EOF {
                     break
                 }
                 continue
             }
             if totalLines >= startLine {
                 var line Line
                 if err := json.Unmarshal(lineBytes, &line); err == nil {
                     lines = append(lines, line)
                 }
             }
             totalLines++
             if err == io.EOF {
                 break
             }
         }
         return lines, totalLines, nil
     }
     ```
     - Already had `ParseFromBytes` and `SliceFromLine` (unchanged)

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of what was consolidated:

**Deleted 5 duplicate parsers:**
- `cli/transcript.go`: `parseTranscript`, `parseTranscriptFromLine`, `parseTranscriptFromBytes`
- `claudecode/transcript.go`: `ParseTranscript`, `parseTranscriptFromLine` + `scannerBufferSize` constant

**Consolidated into 2 shared functions in `transcript/parse.go`:**
- `ParseFromBytes(content)` (already existed)
- `ParseFromFileAtLine(path, startLine)` (new)

**Updated 8 caller files:**
- `hooks_claudecode_handlers.go` - renamed local `transcript` var to `transcriptLines` to avoid shadowing
- `debug.go`, `rewind.go`, `explain_test.go` - switched to `transcript.ParseFromBytes`/`ParseFromFileAtLine`
- `claudecode/claude.go` - 4 calls switched to `transcript.ParseFromBytes`
- `claudecode/transcript.go` - 3 functions switched to `transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine`
- `manual_commit_condensation.go` - switched to `transcript.ParseFromBytes`
- `claudecode/transcript_test.go` - 7 test calls updated
- `integration_test/agent_test.go` - 1 call updated

**Moved 9 parsing tests** from `cli/transcript_test.go` to `transcript/parse_test.go`.

1→// hooks_claudecode_handlers.go contains Claude Code specific hook handler implementations.
     2→// These are called by the hook registry in hook_registry.go.
     3→package cli
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"context"
     7→	"encoding/json"
     8→	"fmt"
     9→	"log/slog"
    10→	"os"
    11→	"path/filepath"
    12→	"strings"
    13→	"time"
    14→
    15→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent"
    16→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode"
    17→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/logging"
    18→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/paths"
    19→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/session"
    20→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/strategy"
    21→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/transcript"
    22→)
    23→
    24→// hookInputData contains parsed hook input and session identifiers.
    25→type hookInputData struct {
    26→	agent     agent.Agent
    27→	input     *agent.HookInput
    28→	sessionID string
    29→}
    30→
    31→// parseAndLogHookInput parses the hook input and sets up logging context.
    32→func parseAndLogHookInput() (*hookInputData, error) {
    33→	// Get the agent from the hook command context (e.g., "entire hooks claude-code ...")
    34→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
    35→	if err != nil {
    36→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
    37→	}
    38→
    39→	// Parse hook input using agent interface
    40→	input, err := ag.ParseHookInput(agent.HookUserPromptSubmit, os.Stdin)
    41→	if err != nil {
    42→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook input: %w", err)
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
    46→	logging.Info(logCtx, "user-prompt-submit",
    47→		slog.String("hook", "user-prompt-submit"),
    48→		slog.String("hook_type", "agent"),
    49→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
    50→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.SessionRef),
    51→	)
    52→
    53→	sessionID := input.SessionID
    54→	if sessionID == "" {
    55→		sessionID = unknownSessionID
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	// Get the Entire session ID, preferring the persisted value to handle midnight boundary
    59→	return &hookInputData{
    60→		agent:     ag,
    61→		input:     input,
    62→		sessionID: sessionID,
    63→	}, nil
    64→}
    65→
    66→// captureInitialState captures the initial state on user prompt submit.
    67→func captureInitialState() error {
    68→	// Parse hook input and setup logging
    69→	hookData, err := parseAndLogHookInput()
    70→	if err != nil {
    71→		return err
    72→	}
    73→
    74→	// CLI captures state directly (including transcript position)
    75→	if err := CapturePrePromptState(hookData.sessionID, hookData.input.SessionRef); err != nil {
    76→		return err
    77→	}
    78→
    79→	// If strategy implements SessionInitializer, call it to initialize session state
    80→	strat := GetStrategy()
    81→
    82→	// Ensure strategy setup is in place (git hooks, gitignore, metadata branch).
    83→	// Done here at turn start so hooks are installed before any mid-turn commits.
    84→	if err := strat.EnsureSetup(); err != nil {
    85→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to ensure strategy setup: %v\n", err)
    86→	}
    87→
    88→	if initializer, ok := strat.(strategy.SessionInitializer); ok {
    89→		agentType := hookData.agent.Type()
    90→		if err := initializer.InitializeSession(hookData.sessionID, agentType, hookData.input.SessionRef, hookData.input.UserPrompt); err != nil {
    91→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to initialize session state: %v\n", err)
    92→		}
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	return nil
    96→}
    97→
    98→// commitWithMetadata commits the session changes with metadata.
    99→func commitWithMetadata() error { //nolint:maintidx // already present in codebase
   100→	// Get the agent for hook input parsing and session ID transformation
   101→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   102→	if err != nil {
   103→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   104→	}
   105→
   106→	// Parse hook input using agent interface
   107→	input, err := ag.ParseHookInput(agent.HookStop, os.Stdin)
   108→	if err != nil {
   109→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook input: %w", err)
   110→	}
   111→
   112→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   113→	logging.Info(logCtx, "stop",
   114→		slog.String("hook", "stop"),
   115→		slog.String("hook_type", "agent"),
   116→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   117→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.SessionRef),
   118→	)
   119→
   120→	sessionID := input.SessionID
   121→	if sessionID == "" {
   122→		sessionID = unknownSessionID
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	transcriptPath := input.SessionRef
   126→	if transcriptPath == "" || !fileExists(transcriptPath) {
   127→		return fmt.Errorf("transcript file not found or empty: %s", transcriptPath)
   128→	}
   129→
   130→	// Early check: bail out quickly if the repo has no commits yet.
   131→	// Without this, the function does a lot of work (transcript copy, prompt extraction, etc.)
   132→	// before eventually failing deep in strategy.SaveChanges().
   133→	if repo, err := strategy.OpenRepository(); err == nil && strategy.IsEmptyRepository(repo) {
   134→		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "Entire: skipping checkpoint. Will activate after first commit.")
   135→		return NewSilentError(strategy.ErrEmptyRepository)
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	// Create session metadata folder using the entire session ID (preserves original date on resume)
   139→	// Use AbsPath to ensure we create at repo root, not relative to cwd
   140→	sessionDir := paths.SessionMetadataDirFromSessionID(sessionID)
   141→	sessionDirAbs, err := paths.AbsPath(sessionDir)
   142→	if err != nil {
   143→		sessionDirAbs = sessionDir // Fallback to relative
   144→	}
   145→	if err := os.MkdirAll(sessionDirAbs, 0o750); err != nil {
   146→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create session directory: %w", err)
   147→	}
   148→
   149→	// Wait for Claude Code to flush the transcript file.
   150→	// The stop hook fires before the transcript is fully written to disk.
   151→	// We poll for our own hook_progress sentinel entry in the file tail,
   152→	// which guarantees all prior entries have been flushed.
   153→	waitForTranscriptFlush(transcriptPath, time.Now())
   154→
   155→	// Copy transcript
   156→	logFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.TranscriptFileName)
   157→	if err := copyFile(transcriptPath, logFile); err != nil {
   158→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to copy transcript: %w", err)
   159→	}
   160→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Copied transcript to: %s\n", sessionDir+"/"+paths.TranscriptFileName)
   161→
   162→	// Load pre-prompt state (captured on UserPromptSubmit)
   163→	// Needed for transcript offset and file change detection
   164→	preState, err := LoadPrePromptState(sessionID)
   165→	if err != nil {
   166→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load pre-prompt state: %v\n", err)
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	// Determine transcript offset: prefer pre-prompt state, fall back to session state.
   170→	// Pre-prompt state has the offset when the transcript path was available at prompt time.
   171→	// Session state has the offset updated after each successful checkpoint save (auto-commit).
   172→	var transcriptOffset int
   173→	if preState != nil && preState.StepTranscriptStart > 0 {
   174→		transcriptOffset = preState.StepTranscriptStart
   175→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Pre-prompt state found: parsing transcript from line %d\n", transcriptOffset)
   176→	} else {
   177→		// Fall back to session state (e.g., auto-commit strategy updates it after each save)
   178→		sessionState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   179→		if loadErr != nil {
   180→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state: %v\n", loadErr)
   181→		}
   182→		if sessionState != nil && sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart > 0 {
   183→			transcriptOffset = sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart
   184→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Session state found: parsing transcript from line %d\n", transcriptOffset)
   185→		}
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	// Parse transcript (optionally from offset for strategies that track transcript position)
   189→	// When transcriptOffset > 0, only parse NEW lines since the last checkpoint
   190→	var transcriptLines []transcriptLine
   191→	var totalLines int
   192→	if transcriptOffset > 0 {
   193→		// Parse only NEW lines since last checkpoint
   194→		transcriptLines, totalLines, err = transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(transcriptPath, transcriptOffset)
   195→		if err != nil {
   196→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse transcript from line %d: %w", transcriptOffset, err)
   197→		}
   198→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Parsed %d new transcript lines (total: %d)\n", len(transcriptLines), totalLines)
   199→	} else {
   200→		// First prompt or no session state - parse entire transcript
   201→		// Use ParseFromFileAtLine with offset 0 to also get totalLines
   202→		transcriptLines, totalLines, err = transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(transcriptPath, 0)
   203→		if err != nil {
   204→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse transcript: %w", err)
   205→		}
   206→	}
   207→
   208→	// Extract all prompts since last checkpoint for prompt file
   209→	allPrompts := extractUserPrompts(transcriptLines)
   210→	promptFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.PromptFileName)
   211→	promptContent := strings.Join(allPrompts, "\n\n---\n\n")
   212→	if err := os.WriteFile(promptFile, []byte(promptContent), 0o600); err != nil {
   213→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write prompt file: %w", err)
   214→	}
   215→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Extracted %d prompt(s) to: %s\n", len(allPrompts), sessionDir+"/"+paths.PromptFileName)
   216→
   217→	// Extract summary
   218→	summaryFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.SummaryFileName)
   219→	summary := extractLastAssistantMessage(transcriptLines)
   220→	if err := os.WriteFile(summaryFile, []byte(summary), 0o600); err != nil {
   221→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write summary file: %w", err)
   222→	}
   223→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Extracted summary to: %s\n", sessionDir+"/"+paths.SummaryFileName)
   224→
   225→	// Get modified files from transcript (main agent + subagents).
   226→	// Subagent transcripts live in <transcriptDir>/<modelSessionID>/subagents/
   227→	subagentsDir := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(transcriptPath), input.SessionID, "subagents")
   228→	modifiedFiles, subagentErr := claudecode.ExtractAllModifiedFiles(transcriptPath, transcriptOffset, subagentsDir)
   229→	if subagentErr != nil {
   230→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to extract modified files with subagents: %v\n", subagentErr)
   231→		// Fall back to main transcript only
   232→		modifiedFiles = extractModifiedFiles(transcriptLines)
   233→	}
   234→
   235→	// Generate commit message from last user prompt
   236→	lastPrompt := ""
   237→	if len(allPrompts) > 0 {
   238→		lastPrompt = allPrompts[len(allPrompts)-1]
   239→	}
   240→	commitMessage := generateCommitMessage(lastPrompt)
   241→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Using commit message: %s\n", commitMessage)
   242→
   243→	// Get repo root for path conversion (not cwd, since Claude may be in a subdirectory)
   244→	// Using cwd would filter out files in sibling directories (paths starting with ..)
   245→	repoRoot, err := paths.RepoRoot()
   246→	if err != nil {
   247→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get repo root: %w", err)
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	if preState != nil {
   251→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Pre-prompt state: %d pre-existing untracked files\n", len(preState.UntrackedFiles))
   252→	}
   253→
   254→	// Compute new and deleted files (single git status call)
   255→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(preState.PreUntrackedFiles())
   256→	if err != nil {
   257→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to compute file changes: %v\n", err)
   258→	}
   259→
   260→	// Filter and normalize all paths (CLI responsibility)
   261→	relModifiedFiles := FilterAndNormalizePaths(modifiedFiles, repoRoot)
   262→	var relNewFiles, relDeletedFiles []string
   263→	if changes != nil {
   264→		relNewFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.New, repoRoot)
   265→		relDeletedFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.Deleted, repoRoot)
   266→	}
   267→
   268→	// Check if there are any changes to commit
   269→	totalChanges := len(relModifiedFiles) + len(relNewFiles) + len(relDeletedFiles)
   270→	if totalChanges == 0 {
   271→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "No files were modified during this session\n")
   272→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Skipping commit\n")
   273→		// Still transition phase even when skipping commit — the turn is ending.
   274→		transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID)
   275→		// Clean up state even when skipping
   276→		if err := CleanupPrePromptState(sessionID); err != nil {
   277→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to cleanup pre-prompt state: %v\n", err)
   278→		}
   279→		return nil
   280→	}
   281→
   282→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files modified during session (%d):\n", len(relModifiedFiles))
   283→	for _, file := range relModifiedFiles {
   284→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   285→	}
   286→	if len(relNewFiles) > 0 {
   287→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "New files created (%d):\n", len(relNewFiles))
   288→		for _, file := range relNewFiles {
   289→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  + %s\n", file)
   290→		}
   291→	}
   292→	if len(relDeletedFiles) > 0 {
   293→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files deleted (%d):\n", len(relDeletedFiles))
   294→		for _, file := range relDeletedFiles {
   295→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   296→		}
   297→	}
   298→
   299→	// Create context file before saving changes
   300→	contextFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.ContextFileName)
   301→	if err := createContextFileMinimal(contextFile, commitMessage, sessionID, promptFile, summaryFile, transcriptLines); err != nil {
   302→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create context file: %w", err)
   303→	}
   304→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Created context file: %s\n", sessionDir+"/"+paths.ContextFileName)
   305→
   306→	// Get git author from local/global config
   307→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   308→	if err != nil {
   309→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get git author: %w", err)
   310→	}
   311→
   312→	// Get the configured strategy
   313→	strat := GetStrategy()
   314→
   315→	// Get agent type from the currently executing hook agent (authoritative source)
   316→	var agentType agent.AgentType
   317→	if hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent(); agentErr == nil {
   318→		agentType = hookAgent.Type()
   319→	}
   320→
   321→	// Get transcript position from pre-prompt state (captured at step/turn start)
   322→	var transcriptIdentifierAtStart string
   323→	var transcriptLinesAtStart int
   324→	if preState != nil {
   325→		transcriptIdentifierAtStart = preState.LastTranscriptIdentifier
   326→		transcriptLinesAtStart = preState.StepTranscriptStart
   327→	}
   328→
   329→	// Calculate token usage for this checkpoint (Claude Code specific)
   330→	var tokenUsage *agent.TokenUsage
   331→	if transcriptPath != "" {
   332→		usage, tokenErr := claudecode.CalculateTotalTokenUsage(transcriptPath, transcriptLinesAtStart, subagentsDir)
   333→		if tokenErr != nil {
   334→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to calculate token usage: %v\n", tokenErr)
   335→		} else {
   336→			tokenUsage = usage
   337→		}
   338→	}
   339→
   340→	// Build fully-populated save context and delegate to strategy
   341→	ctx := strategy.SaveContext{
   342→		SessionID:                sessionID,
   343→		ModifiedFiles:            relModifiedFiles,
   344→		NewFiles:                 relNewFiles,
   345→		DeletedFiles:             relDeletedFiles,
   346→		MetadataDir:              sessionDir,
   347→		MetadataDirAbs:           sessionDirAbs,
   348→		CommitMessage:            commitMessage,
   349→		TranscriptPath:           transcriptPath,
   350→		AuthorName:               author.Name,
   351→		AuthorEmail:              author.Email,
   352→		AgentType:                agentType,
   353→		StepTranscriptIdentifier: transcriptIdentifierAtStart,
   354→		StepTranscriptStart:      transcriptLinesAtStart,
   355→		TokenUsage:               tokenUsage,
   356→	}
   357→
   358→	if err := strat.SaveChanges(ctx); err != nil {
   359→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save changes: %w", err)
   360→	}
   361→
   362→	// Update session state with new transcript position for strategies that create
   363→	// commits on the active branch (auto-commit strategy). This prevents parsing old transcript
   364→	// lines on subsequent checkpoints.
   365→	// Note: Shadow strategy tracks transcript position per-step via StepTranscriptStart in
   366→	// pre-prompt state, but doesn't advance CheckpointTranscriptStart in session state because
   367→	// its checkpoints accumulate all files touched across the entire session.
   368→	if strat.Name() == strategy.StrategyNameAutoCommit {
   369→		// Load session state for updating transcript position
   370→		sessionState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   371→		if loadErr != nil {
   372→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state: %v\n", loadErr)
   373→		}
   374→		// Create session state lazily if it doesn't exist (backward compat for resumed sessions
   375→		// or if InitializeSession was never called/failed)
   376→		if sessionState == nil {
   377→			sessionState = &strategy.SessionState{
   378→				SessionID: sessionID,
   379→			}
   380→		}
   381→		sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart = totalLines
   382→		sessionState.StepCount++
   383→		if updateErr := strategy.SaveSessionState(sessionState); updateErr != nil {
   384→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update session state: %v\n", updateErr)
   385→		} else {
   386→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Updated session state: transcript position=%d, checkpoint=%d\n",
   387→				totalLines, sessionState.StepCount)
   388→		}
   389→	}
   390→
   391→	// Fire EventTurnEnd to transition session phase (all strategies).
   392→	// This moves ACTIVE → IDLE.
   393→	transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID)
   394→
   395→	// Clean up pre-prompt state (CLI responsibility)
   396→	if err := CleanupPrePromptState(sessionID); err != nil {
   397→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to cleanup pre-prompt state: %v\n", err)
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	return nil
   401→}
   402→
   403→// handleClaudeCodePostTodo handles the PostToolUse[TodoWrite] hook for subagent checkpoints.
   404→// Creates a checkpoint if we're in a subagent context (active pre-task file exists).
   405→// Skips silently if not in subagent context (main agent).
   406→func handleClaudeCodePostTodo() error {
   407→	input, err := parseSubagentCheckpointHookInput(os.Stdin)
   408→	if err != nil {
   409→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse PostToolUse[TodoWrite] input: %w", err)
   410→	}
   411→
   412→	// Get agent for logging context
   413→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   414→	if err != nil {
   415→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   416→	}
   417→
   418→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   419→	logging.Info(logCtx, "post-todo",
   420→		slog.String("hook", "post-todo"),
   421→		slog.String("hook_type", "subagent"),
   422→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   423→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.TranscriptPath),
   424→		slog.String("tool_use_id", input.ToolUseID),
   425→	)
   426→
   427→	// Check if we're in a subagent context by looking for an active pre-task file
   428→	taskToolUseID, found := FindActivePreTaskFile()
   429→	if !found {
   430→		// Not in subagent context - this is a main agent TodoWrite, skip
   431→		return nil
   432→	}
   433→
   434→	// Skip on default branch to avoid polluting main/master history
   435→	if skip, branchName := ShouldSkipOnDefaultBranch(); skip {
   436→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Entire: skipping incremental checkpoint on branch '%s'\n", branchName)
   437→		return nil
   438→	}
   439→
   440→	// Detect file changes since last checkpoint
   441→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(nil)
   442→	if err != nil {
   443→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to detect changed files: %v\n", err)
   444→		return nil
   445→	}
   446→
   447→	// If no file changes, skip creating a checkpoint
   448→	if len(changes.Modified) == 0 && len(changes.New) == 0 && len(changes.Deleted) == 0 {
   449→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] No file changes detected, skipping incremental checkpoint\n")
   450→		return nil
   451→	}
   452→
   453→	// Get git author
   454→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   455→	if err != nil {
   456→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to get git author: %v\n", err)
   457→		return nil
   458→	}
   459→
   460→	// Get the active strategy
   461→	strat := GetStrategy()
   462→
   463→	// Get the session ID from the transcript path or input, then transform to Entire session ID
   464→	sessionID := input.SessionID
   465→	if sessionID == "" {
   466→		sessionID = paths.ExtractSessionIDFromTranscriptPath(input.TranscriptPath)
   467→	}
   468→
   469→	// Get next checkpoint sequence
   470→	seq := GetNextCheckpointSequence(sessionID, taskToolUseID)
   471→
   472→	// Extract the todo content from the tool_input.
   473→	// PostToolUse receives the NEW todo list where the just-completed work is
   474→	// marked as "completed". The last completed item is the work that was just done.
   475→	todoContent := ExtractLastCompletedTodoFromToolInput(input.ToolInput)
   476→	if todoContent == "" {
   477→		// No completed items - this is likely the first TodoWrite (planning phase).
   478→		// Check if there are any todos at all to avoid duplicate messages.
   479→		todoCount := CountTodosFromToolInput(input.ToolInput)
   480→		if todoCount > 0 {
   481→			// Use "Planning: N todos" format for the first TodoWrite
   482→			todoContent = fmt.Sprintf("Planning: %d todos", todoCount)
   483→		}
   484→		// If todoCount == 0, todoContent remains empty and FormatIncrementalMessage
   485→		// will fall back to "Checkpoint #N" format
   486→	}
   487→
   488→	// Get agent type from the currently executing hook agent (authoritative source)
   489→	var agentType agent.AgentType
   490→	if hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent(); agentErr == nil {
   491→		agentType = hookAgent.Type()
   492→	}
   493→
   494→	// Build incremental checkpoint context
   495→	ctx := strategy.TaskCheckpointContext{
   496→		SessionID:           sessionID,
   497→		ToolUseID:           taskToolUseID,
   498→		ModifiedFiles:       changes.Modified,
   499→		NewFiles:            changes.New,
   500→		DeletedFiles:        changes.Deleted,
   501→		TranscriptPath:      input.TranscriptPath,
   502→		AuthorName:          author.Name,
   503→		AuthorEmail:         author.Email,
   504→		IsIncremental:       true,
   505→		IncrementalSequence: seq,
   506→		IncrementalType:     input.ToolName,
   507→		IncrementalData:     input.ToolInput,
   508→		TodoContent:         todoContent,
   509→		AgentType:           agentType,
   510→	}
   511→
   512→	// Save incremental checkpoint
   513→	if err := strat.SaveTaskCheckpoint(ctx); err != nil {
   514→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to save incremental checkpoint: %v\n", err)
   515→		return nil
   516→	}
   517→
   518→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] Created incremental checkpoint #%d for %s (task: %s)\n",
   519→		seq, input.ToolName, taskToolUseID[:min(12, len(taskToolUseID))])
   520→	return nil
   521→}
